title: Purr-fect Day for Cat Lovers Everywhere
story: Cat lovers around the world celebrate their furry friends every August 8 on International Cat Day! People post adorable photos of their cats on social media, share funny cat videos, and spoil their pets with extra treats and toys. Animal shelters use this special day to promote cat adoption and educate people about proper cat care. Many cat cafes offer special deals, and some people even dress up like their favorite feline. Whether your cat is a playful kitten or a lazy lap cat, today is all about showing appreciation for these independent, mysterious, and lovable companions who rule our homes with their purrs and paws.
activity_question: What do animal shelters often do on International Cat Day?
activity_choices: Teach cats to bark like dogs
follow_up_question: What makes cats such special and unique pets compared to other animals?

title: Sneaky Zucchini Surprise Day!
story: People across America grab bags of zucchini and sneak them onto their neighbors' porches every August 8! This hilarious holiday started because gardeners grow way too much zucchini and need to get rid of it somehow. On this day, you might find mysterious bags of green vegetables waiting by your front door. Some people ring the doorbell and run away giggling. Others leave funny notes like "You've been zucchini-ed!" It's like a vegetable prank that actually helps everyone eat healthier.
activity_question: What do people typically do with zucchini on this holiday?
bonus_fact: Some people leave notes saying "You've been zucchini-ed!" with their vegetable gifts.

title: Happiness Happens Day: Spread the Joy!
story: Every August 8, people around the world celebrate National Happiness Happens Day by doing one simple thing: spreading joy wherever they go! This special day encourages everyone to share smiles, compliments, and acts of kindness with friends, family, and even strangers. People post happy photos on social media, write thank-you notes, give surprise hugs, and do random acts of kindness like buying coffee for the person behind them in line. Some schools and workplaces organize happiness challenges where everyone tries to make someone else smile. The best part? Happiness is contagious, so when you make one person happy, they often pass it on to someone else!
activity_question: What do people often do on National Happiness Happens Day to spread joy?
activity_choices: Write thank-you notes and give surprise hugs
activity_answer: Write thank-you notes and give surprise hugs
bonus_fact: Scientists say that smiling actually makes your brain release chemicals that make you feel happier!

title: Forever and Ever: International Infinity Day
story: Every August 8, math lovers around the world celebrate International Infinity Day! This special day was chosen because when you turn the number 8 sideways, it looks exactly like the infinity symbol (∞). People celebrate by sharing infinity jokes on social media, creating sideways-8 art projects, and discussing mind-bending concepts like endless numbers and limitless space. Teachers use this day to make math fun by explaining how infinity works in cool ways. Some families even bake figure-8 cookies and turn them sideways while eating them. It's the perfect day to think about things that go on forever and ever!
activity_question: Why was August 8 chosen for International Infinity Day?
activity_choices: Because the number 8 turned sideways looks like the infinity symbol
Because it's the 8th month and infinity has 8 letters
Because mathematicians like the number 8 best
Because August has infinite hot days
activity_answer: Because the number 8 turned sideways looks like the infinity symbol
bonus_fact: The infinity symbol was first used by mathematician John Wallis in 1655!
